sourceMovie.title: Thérèse
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Thérèse (2012) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Chicago
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thérèse clicked for you, Chicago (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thérèse clicked for you, La Vie en Rose (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thérèse?
faq.answer: Try Chicago, La Vie en Rose, The Cider House Rules—each shares DNA with Thérèse while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thérèse?
faq.answer: Thérèse (2012) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thérèse worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
